conf-proc-title-group.conf-normalized-title: Energy Efficiency and Agricultural Engineering (EE&AE), 2025 10th International Conference on

conf-meta.conf-name: 2025 10th International Conference on Energy Efficiency and Agricultural Engineering (EE&AE)

title-group.article-title: Innodiversification Approach to Modeling the Sustainability of Organizations

institution-wrap.institution: Siberian University of Consumer Cooperation, Kazan Cooperative Institute (branch) of the Russian, University of Cooperation
Siberian Institute of Management of the Russian Academy of National Economy and Public Administration

permissions.copyright-statement: 979-8-3315-1299-6 © 2025 IEEE. All rights reserved, including rights for text and data mining, and training of artificial intelligence and similar technologies. Personal use is permitted, but republication/redistribution requires IEEE permission. See https://www.ieee.org/publications/rights/index.html for more information.

abstract.p: The article is devoted to the development of a methodology for predicting the sustainability and efficiency of organizations based on an innodiversification approach. The authors offer a comprehensive model integrating multifactorial analysis, linear regression and machine learning, which allows taking into account both internal and external factors affecting the activities of organizations. The article presents the stages of model construction, including data collection, preprocessing, analysis, and forecasting. Special attention is paid to the advantages of an innovative diversification approach that ensures the sustainable development of organizations in the context of global economic instability, which is directly relevant to the conference's focus on energy efficiency and sustainable development in agro-industrial and related sectors.

sec.label: I.
sec.title: Introduction (Heading 1)
sec.p: The relevance of the research is determined by the need to increase the sustainability and efficiency of organizations in the context of global economic instability, growing competition, and the increasing importance of energy and resource efficiency [1]. Modern organizations, particularly in sectors related to agriculture and engineering, face multifaceted challenges related to volatile market conditions, rapid technological innovations, and the imperative to integrate social and environmental responsibilities into their core strategies. The search for robust methodological tools to forecast organizational performance under such conditions remains a pressing issue in economic science [2], [3].

Analysis of the current state of research reveals significant contributions in the fields of sustainability modeling and performance forecasting. Traditional methods, such as financial analysis [7], cost-benefit analysis [3], [4], and SWOT-analysis [8], provide valuable insights but often lack a holistic view, overlooking the synergistic effects between innovation, diversification, and long-term resilience. Furthermore, the application of advanced analytical techniques, including machine learning, for forecasting in the context of regional economic systems is an area of growing interest [9], [10]. This study aims to contribute to this discourse by justifying and integrating an innodiversification approach with modern quantitative methods.

The purpose of this research is to justify and refine a methodology for constructing a forecasting model for the sustainability and efficiency of organizations. This involves a critical comparative analysis of existing methods, the synthesis of an innodiversification-based framework, and the integration of multifactorial analysis, linear regression, and machine learning techniques to enhance predictive accuracy and practical applicability for managerial decision-making.

sec.label: II.
sec.title: Research Methodology
sec.p: The proposed methodology for constructing a model for predicting the sustainability and efficiency of organizations is presented in Figure 1. It synthesizes elements from established approaches [3]–[8] into a new, integrated framework centered on the innodiversification paradigm.

In the context of global economic instability and growing competition, organizations of organizations face the need to increase their sustainability and efficiency. This requires the development of a reliable methodology for forecasting key indicators, which will make it possible to make informed management decisions. In this paper, we propose a hypothesis of a methodology for building a model for predicting the sustainability and effectiveness of organizations.
The hypothesis of the methodology is based on the integration of multifactorial analysis, linear regression, machine learning, data clustering and comparative analysis of techniques.
The algorithm of the technique includes five fundamental steps, detailed below.

sec.label: A.
sec.title: Comparison of Methods and Justification of the Innodiversification Approach
sec.p: This initial stage involves a systematic comparison of various methods for selecting and justifying indicators. Based on a comparative analysis (see Table I), the innodiversification approach was selected due to its comprehensive nature, which integrates innovativeness, diversification, and a multi-faceted assessment of sustainability and efficiency.

sec.label: B.
sec.title: Data Collection and Preprocessing
sec.p: The second stage entails collecting data on key indicators of sustainability and efficiency of organizations. These data may include financial, industrial, social, and environmental metrics. The collected data subsequently undergo preprocessing, including clustering, to ensure quality and consistency for subsequent analysis.

sec.label: C.
sec.title: Multifactorial Analysis
sec.p: This stage focuses on identifying key internal and external factors affecting the sustainability and efficiency of organizations. The analysis of correlations between these factors and target indicators allows for the determination of the most significant influences and their dynamics over time.

sec.label: D.
sec.title: Linear Regression and Machine Learning
sec.p: At this stage, linear regression models are constructed for key indicators. The models are evaluated for accuracy using metrics such as the coefficient of determination ($\mathrm{R}^{2}$) and the Mean Absolute Error (MAE). The methodology also incorporates machine learning techniques to account for possible non-linear dependencies and complex interactions between factors, thereby enhancing the robustness of the forecasting model.

sec.label: E.
sec.title: Integration of Results
sec.p:
The final stage involves the integration of results from regression and machine learning models to produce a comprehensive forecast. The forecasts are interpreted considering economic theory and the practical context of the organizations' activities, enabling the formulation of informed management decisions and development scenarios.
Fig. 1.
Methodology for constructing a forecasting model for the sustainability and efficiency of organizations

sec.label: III.
sec.title: Justification of the Methodology
sec.p: Integrated approach: the methodology integrates multifactorial analysis, linear regression and machine learning, which allows you to take into account a variety of factors and dependencies. This ensures more accurate and reliable forecasting. Taking into account non-linear dependencies makes it possible to identify complex and non-linear relationships between factors and targets. This increases the accuracy of forecasts and their adequacy to real conditions. Cross-validation of models ensures their reliability and stability. This helps to increase confidence in forecasts. The forecasting results can be used to make informed management decisions. This helps to increase the sustainability and effectiveness of organizations. The proposed methodology for constructing a forecasting model for the sustainability and effectiveness of organizations is based on the integration of multifactorial analysis, linear and nonlinear regression. This allows you to take into account a variety of factors and dependencies, ensuring the accuracy and reliability of forecasts. The implementation of this methodology helps to increase the competitiveness and sustainability of organizations in the context of global economic instability.

The selection and justification of indicators of sustainability and effectiveness of organizations are key tasks to ensure their long-term development and competitiveness. The selection and justification of indicators of sustainability and efficiency of organizations require the use of comprehensive analysis methods that take into account the specifics of the industry and modern economic conditions. There are many techniques that can be used for this purpose, including financial analysis, cost-benefit analysis, value chain analysis, SWOT analysis, and innodiversification approach.

The selection and justification of indicators for organizational sustainability and efficiency are critical for ensuring long-term development and competitiveness. This requires comprehensive analysis methods that account for industry specifics and contemporary economic conditions. Table I presents a comparison of various methodologies.
Table I. Comparison of methods for selecting and justifying indicators of sustainability and effectiveness of organizations
Methodology Description Advantages Disadvantages
Cost-benefit analysis [3], [4] Involves estimating the costs and benefits of projects or strategies. Evaluates economic feasibility of solutions. Limited to economic aspects; ignores long-term social/environmen tal consequences.
Value chain analysis [5] Identifies key stages of value creation for optimization. End-product orientation, focuses on process optimization. Lacks consideration of innovative changes and the external environment.
Innodiversificati on Approach [6], [9] Assesses innovative and diversificatio n strategies for sustainable development Comprehensi ve; covers financial, social, environmental, and innovative aspects. Requires significant resources; can be complex to implement.
Financial analysis [7], [10] Assesses financial indicators (profitability, liquidity, stability). Simple, accessible, provides quantitative assessment. Limited perspective on development prospects; insufficient attention to intangibles and innovation.
SWOT-analysis [8] Assesses internal (Strengths, Weaknesses) and external (Opportuniti es, Threats) factors. Provides a comprehensiv e qualitative understanding Subjective; not always suitable for quantitative assessment.

As a result of a comparative analysis of the five most well-known methods that can be used to select and justify indicators of sustainability and effectiveness of organizations, an innodiversification approach was preferred. The choice of this approach was based on the advantages of this approach shown in Figure 2 [9]–[10].

The innodiversification approach was preferred due to its integrated nature, which allows organizations to adapt to changing market conditions, enhance competitiveness, and ensure long-term sustainability. Its core advantages are innovativeness, diversification, an integrated approach, social and environmental responsibility, and a focus on sustainable development.

Applying this approach to the consumer cooperation system in the Novosibirsk region, indicators were grouped into three blocks, resulting in the selection of 36 sustainability indicators and 8 efficiency indicators for the period from 2014 to 2023. The analysis revealed a significant improvement in both sustainability and effectiveness over this decade.

As a result of the innodiversification analysis, using the example of the consumer cooperation of the Novosibirsk region, the indicators were grouped into three blocks characterizing the results of the industry, including a period of time, 36 indicators of sustainability and 8 indicators of efficiency.
Over the period from 2014 to 2023, there has been a significant improvement in the sustainability and effectiveness of consumer cooperation.

sec.label: A.
sec.title: Sustainability Indicators
sec.p:
The analysis of key sustainability indicators demonstrated positive dynamics:
•
Growth in Asset Value: the average annual cost of fixed assets (buildings, machinery, vehicles) and working capital showed a substantial increase, indicating a strengthening material and technical base. For instance, the value of buildings and structures (Y1) grew by 2.67 times. •
Improved Financial Stability: key financial metrics improved significantly. The average annual amount of funds (Y5) increased markedly. The financial independence coefficient (Y14) rose to 0.94, indicating higher resilience. Liquidity ratios (Y19, Y20, Y21) also showed positive dynamics. •
Human Resources Development: the average age of staff (Y6) decreased, while the average salary (Y8) increased from 14,783 to 35,797 rubles, confirming improved working conditions and incentives.
Fig. 2.
Advantages of the innodiversification approach

sec.label: B.
sec.title: Efficiency Indicators
sec.p:
The analysis of efficiency revealed a mixed but generally positive trend:
•
Increased Operational Efficiency: labor productivity (E3) and the volume of activity per shareholder (E2) increased substantially, indicating more effective use of resources. •
Profitability Dynamics: return on assets (E5) and return on equity (E8) showed significant growth, demonstrating enhanced profitability. However, the return on capital (E1) decreased, signaling a need for optimized asset utilization despite their overall growth.

list-item.p: The average annual amount of funds (Y5) increased from 7,360.5 thousand rubles to $64,324.5$ thousand rubles.
Average salary (Y8): increases from 14,783 rubles in 2014 to 35,797 rubles in 2023, which confirms the improvement in working conditions.
Financial independence coefficient (Y14): increases to 0.94, which indicates a higher financial stability of organizations.
The liquidity ratios (Y19, Y20, Y21) also showed positive dynamics, which indicates an increase in financial stability.
The average age of staff (Y6) decreased from 52.5 years to 46.4 years, which may indicate a restructuring of the staff.
The level of education (Y7) remained stable, which indicates the maintenance of staff qualifications.
The average salary (Y8) increased from 14,783 rubles to 35,797 rubles, indicating an improvement in working conditions.

Return on capital (E1): decreases from 1.94 in 2014 to 1.63 in 2023, which may be due to an increase in the cost of fixed assets, which indicates the need to pay attention to the efficiency of asset use.
The volume of activity per shareholder (E2) increased from 2.86 to 59.48, which indicates an increase in the activity of shareholders.
Labor productivity (E3) increased from 9.57 to 27.56, which indicates an increase in the efficiency of using labor resources and indicates dynamic development.
Return on assets (E5): increases by 98.74 % in 2023, demonstrating an increase in profitability.
Return on equity (E8) has increased significantly, indicating an increase in profitability.

p.italic: Based on the analysis, three key trends were identified:

list-item.p: Growth of Sustainability: A significant increase in the value of fixed assets and cash reserves, creating a foundation for further expansion.
Improving Working Conditions: A rise in average wages and stable educational levels, contributing to higher productivity.
Need for Optimization: A decrease in return on capital necessitates more efficient management of financial and material resources.

p.italic: Based on the analysis of current trends, the following forecasts are proposed for the next 5 years:

list-item.p: The value of fixed and working capital will continue to grow, further strengthening the material base.
Financial stability will improve, with the financial independence coefficient potentially exceeding 1.
The average age of staff will decrease further, and the average salary is projected to rise to 42,000 rubles, improving qualifications and attracting talent.
Efficiency metrics are expected to improve, with return on capital (E1) projected to grow to 2.0, and returns on assets and equity to show steady increases.

sec.label: V.
sec.title: Conclusion
sec.p: The practical significance lies in the possibility of applying the proposed methodology to make informed management decisions. The model allows you to predict the key performance indicators of organizations, which helps to increase their competitiveness and sustainability in the long term.

The proposed methodology based on the innodiversification approach makes it possible to effectively predict the sustainability and effectiveness of organizations. The main advantages of the technique include:
•
Consideration of a wide range of factors, including financial, social, environmental and innovative aspects. •
The ability to adapt to changing market conditions. •
Improve forecast accuracy by integrating multifactor analysis and machine learning.

The study justifies a methodology for forecasting organizational sustainability and efficiency based on the innodiversification approach. The main advantages of the proposed methodology include its ability to consider a wide range of factors, adapt to changing market conditions, and improve forecast accuracy through the integration of multifactor analysis and machine learning.

The practical significance lies in the model's applicability for making informed management decisions aimed at enhancing the competitiveness and long-term sustainability of organizations, particularly in sectors critical to regional development, such as consumer cooperation.

Further development of the methodology will be aimed at incorporating additional factors, such as the impact of digitalization and global economic trends. It is recommended to test the methodology on real data from various organizations and to conduct a comparative analysis of forecasting results against other methods to quantitatively assess its advantages.
The proposed innodiversification approach has high practical value and can be recommended for use in management practice.

ref.mixed-citation: M. Chernyakov et al., “Management by objectives: modeling the investment policy of service enterprises from the perspective of the digital economy,” 9th Int. Conf. EE&AE, Ruse, 2024. DOI: 10.1109/EEAE60309.2024.10600598.
J. F. Lamb, “Sustainability and performance in agribusiness: A review of integrated approaches,” Journal of Cleaner Production, vol. 305, 2021.
E. Boardman, A. Greenberg, D. Vining, “Cost-Benefit Analysis: Concepts and Practice,” 5th ed., Cambridge University Press, 2018.
Yu. A. Rakhmatullina et al., “The cost-benefit concept in the economic assessment of the effectiveness of investments in public projects,” Discussion, no. 4(113), pp. 42–50, 2022.
M. E. Porter, “Competitive Advantage: Creating and Sustaining Superior Performance,” Free Press, 1985.
M. M. Chernyakova, “Innodiversification approach to regulating the process of transformation of the dairy industry into the digital economy,” Novosibirsk, NSTU, 2020.
A. N. Zhikina, “Financial analysis: textbook and workshop for universities,” Yurayt, 2024.
A. S. Vorobyeva, “SWOT analysis as the main research method in management,” Synergy of Sciences, no. 62, pp. 244–251, 2021.

V. M. Chernyakov, “The methodology of forecasting the results of the agro-industrial complex of the region,” Herald of SIBIT, vol. 13, no. 4, pp. 155–160, 2024. https://doi.org/10.24412/2225-8264-2024-4-878.
T. Hill, P. Lewicki, “Statistics: Methods and Applications. A Comprehensive Reference for Science, Industry, and Data Mining,” StatSoft, 2007.
